_meta.purpose: Marketing firewall deny-list. The path prefixes the OPERATOR owns and rewrites. The engine never edits these.
_meta.enforcement: WIRED 2026-06-26 (Phase-21 closeout). engine/hooks/guard-marketing.js is registered as a PreToolUse Write|Edit|MultiEdit hook in .claude/settings.json and reads this file to block any Write/Edit/MultiEdit whose target matches off_limits[]. The guard resolves the target path from argv → $CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_PATH → stdin tool_input.file_path, and fails OPEN if no path or deny-list is readable (engine work is never bricked). Note: a Claude Code session loads hook config at startup, so the physical block takes effect on the next session reload; the executing agent honors the deny-list directly in the meantime.
_meta.to_unwire: Remove the PreToolUse guard-marketing.js entry from .claude/settings.json hooks and flip _meta.enforcement back to STAGED (operator's 'let holes show on first run' mode).

off_limits_after_split._note: These become off-limits once the A1/A3 extractions land. Path-level guards can't sub-target line ranges, so the WHOLE file flips to marketing-owned only after its wiring is extracted out.
pending: prompts/step1-light-pass.md (marketing half — after line-223 split + retire to _quarantine)
.claude/skills/*/AGENT-*.md (the judgment prompts produced by the A3 SKILL split)

coordinate._note: Still MIXED — contain wiring not yet extracted. Edit only to perform the approved extraction; never to change marketing content.
